nodes.label: Foundations Schema and Reference Data Phase
nodes.rationale: Establish the full load-bearing schema and reference data before downstream space, FX, account, and dashboard slices.
nodes.norm_label: foundations schema and reference data phase

nodes.label: Curated Currency Reference Data
nodes.rationale: A small seeded table guarantees that every stored currency is supported by the later FX layer.

nodes.label: Six Major Currency Seed
nodes.rationale: USD, CNY, EUR, GBP, JPY, and HKD were selected because all are Frankfurter-convertible.

nodes.label: ISO 4217 Minor-Unit Exponent
nodes.rationale: The currency row is the authority for decimal placement, including JPY with exponent zero.

nodes.label: Integer Minor-Unit Money
nodes.rationale: Integer storage avoids IEEE-754 drift and a per-currency exponent avoids corrupting zero-decimal currencies.

nodes.label: Stable Surrogate Channel ID and Foreign Key
nodes.rationale: ID-based references preserve historical attribution across channel renames.
nodes.norm_label: stable surrogate channel id and foreign key

nodes.label: Uniform Channel Soft Delete
nodes.rationale: One reversible archive path preserves foreign-key integrity and avoids a delete-if-unused hybrid.

nodes.label: Static ISO 3166 Alpha-2 Countries
nodes.rationale: Fixed country codes prevent free-text fragmentation in later dashboard grouping.

nodes.label: Reserved Space FX Snapshot Columns
nodes.rationale: Declaring rate_used, rate_as_of, rate_source, and amount_usd before space CRUD avoids a later backfill migration.

nodes.label: RSC Reads and Server Actions Write
nodes.rationale: Direct server-side reads and Server Actions avoid an unnecessary client fetch or REST layer for a single-user app.
nodes.norm_label: rsc reads and server actions write

nodes.label: Committed Generate-and-Migrate Flow
nodes.rationale: Generated SQL plus programmatic migrate provides reviewable evidence for the passing-migration success criterion.

nodes.label: better-sqlite3 Node Runtime Constraint
nodes.rationale: The native SQLite module must stay on the Node runtime and should use an LTS Node ABI to avoid source compilation failures.

nodes.label: Neutral Archive Confirmation
nodes.rationale: Archive is reversible, so red destructive styling is reserved for the later irreversible cascade delete.

nodes.label: Exchange-Rate Layer Phase
nodes.rationale: Build a persistent and resilient FX source before any USD-aware space exists, avoiding an amount_usd backfill.

nodes.label: FX-01 External Fetch and Local Cache
nodes.norm_label: fx-01 external fetch and local cache

nodes.label: FX-03 Cached Fallback and Stale Flag
nodes.norm_label: fx-03 cached fallback and stale flag

nodes.label: Persistent fx_rate Cache
nodes.rationale: SQLite is the cache so rates survive application restarts without Redis or a separate cache service.

nodes.label: One FX Row per Currency
nodes.rationale: A currency-keyed row supports direct Phase 3 lookup and avoids JSON parsing or latest-snapshot queries.

nodes.label: X-to-USD Decimal-String Rates
nodes.rationale: Normalizing to USD per one unit of currency makes Phase 3 conversion a multiply and keeps rates out of float columns.

nodes.label: Frankfurter Anti-Corruption Service
nodes.rationale: A single module owns fetch, validation, direction normalization, persistence, and fallback so external response details cannot leak across the app.

nodes.label: Zod External-Response Boundary
nodes.rationale: The external API response is untrusted and must be checked for shape plus positive finite rate values before any write.

nodes.label: USD-to-X Rate Inversion
nodes.rationale: Frankfurter returns X per USD, so the service inverts once at the write boundary to store USD per X.

nodes.label: upsertRates Atomic Multi-Row Upsert
nodes.rationale: All currency rows update inside one Drizzle transaction so a partial cache set is never committed.

nodes.label: Last-Good Cache Fallback
nodes.rationale: On timeout, HTTP failure, or invalid data, the service leaves the database untouched and returns the existing cache.

nodes.label: Failure-Only Request-Scoped Stale Flag
nodes.rationale: Stale means a refresh failed and cached values were served; cache age only decides whether to attempt refresh.

nodes.label: Lazy Age-Gated Auto Refresh
nodes.rationale: A view refreshes only when the cache is empty or roughly one day old, matching Frankfurter's daily publication cadence.

nodes.label: Four-Second Blocking Fetch Timeout
nodes.rationale: A bounded blocking request degrades to cache rather than holding the page on a slow or hung API.

nodes.label: refreshRates Server Action
nodes.rationale: The action takes no client input; the trust boundary remains the service-side API response validation.

nodes.label: Reference Data Rates Screen
nodes.rationale: A dedicated screen makes the FX layer independently visible and testable before the dashboard exists.

nodes.label: Empty Cache Unavailable State
nodes.rationale: An empty cache plus failed fetch shows an explicit unavailable state instead of crashing or fabricating zero rates.

nodes.label: Wall-Clock fetched_at Semantics
nodes.rationale: Fetch time drives both the refresh-age check and the displayed as-of label; the optional publication date was omitted for MVP.

nodes.label: open.er-api.com Secondary Fallback Deferred
nodes.rationale: Keeping one live source avoids validating and maintaining a second external response shape in the MVP.

nodes.label: FX Rate History Deferred
nodes.rationale: Append-only history and latest-row selection are unnecessary until audit history becomes a real requirement.

nodes.label: Drizzle Transaction API Correction
nodes.rationale: Drizzle executes db.transaction(callback) immediately and requires writes through the tx handle; the curried better-sqlite3 pattern was incorrect here.

nodes.label: Optional rateDate Column Omitted
nodes.rationale: The cache contract only needs rateToUsd and fetchedAt; publication date can be added later without a backfill.

nodes.label: Partial Frankfurter Response Completeness Warning
nodes.rationale: A record schema accepts missing requested currencies, allowing a partial refresh to appear fully fresh.

nodes.label: Unreachable Refresh Action Failure Variant
nodes.rationale: refreshFromApi catches all service failures, so the action's ok:false branch and matching UI branch are dead unless unexpected errors are surfaced.

nodes.label: Exponential Rate String Warning
nodes.rationale: toPrecision can emit scientific notation for extreme rates, violating a plain trimmed-decimal expectation for future currencies.

nodes.label: Phase 2 UAT Five of Five Passed
nodes.norm_label: phase 2 uat five of five passed

nodes.label: Dashboard and Overview Phase
nodes.rationale: Deliver renewal risk and frozen USD spend together as the root working surface of the internal tool.

nodes.label: Single Dashboard Query Facade
nodes.rationale: Centralizing all aggregates in one explicit-db facade gives the root page and tests one stable accounting contract.

nodes.label: Stored Expiry-Date Window
nodes.rationale: The dashboard filters stored expiry dates using the existing seven-day status rule rather than recalculating subscription periods.

nodes.label: Frozen USD Cost Total
nodes.rationale: Historical dashboard totals use stored USD minor-unit snapshots and never live FX recomputation.

nodes.label: Independent Space and Child Cost Aggregates
nodes.rationale: Space payments and child monthly costs are summed separately before addition so one-to-many joins cannot multiply parent values.
nodes.norm_label: independent space and child cost aggregates

nodes.label: Parent-Child Double-Counting Guard
nodes.rationale: A multi-child test locks the rule that adding children cannot duplicate the space payment subtotal.

nodes.label: Integer-Minor Distribution Reconciliation
nodes.rationale: Every dimension is built from the same stored integer USD cost classes and must sum exactly to the displayed grand total.

nodes.label: Space Child Status and Seat-Type Counts
nodes.norm_label: space child status and seat-type counts

nodes.label: Dynamic Root Dashboard Server Component
nodes.rationale: The SQLite-backed root route stays on the Node runtime and opts out of static rendering.

nodes.label: View-First Dashboard
nodes.rationale: The overview links to existing space flows but introduces no dashboard-specific edit, delete, or payment mutations.

nodes.label: CSS Bar-List Distributions
nodes.rationale: Existing cards and fixed-height CSS bars communicate distributions without a chart dependency.

nodes.label: Four-Metric Operations Order
nodes.rationale: Renewal risk, frozen USD cost, space count, and child count appear in that exact scan order.

nodes.label: First-Viewport Renewal Priority
nodes.rationale: The heading, four metrics, and start of the expiring-space section must be visible before distribution analysis.

nodes.label: No Fake Zero Loading Metrics
nodes.rationale: Server-render real values or skeletons; zero is business data, not a safe loading placeholder.

nodes.label: Child Account Same-Day Email Reminders
nodes.rationale: Due-day reminders cover non-self child accounts and can notify both the owner and an optional matching subscription recipient.

nodes.label: Per-Child Due-Date Recipient Idempotency
nodes.rationale: A reminder is uniquely guarded by child account, due date, and recipient email so scheduler retries do not duplicate mail.

nodes.label: Layered Single-User Monolith
nodes.rationale: A single-user accounting tool needs one deployable app and relational store, not microservices, queues, roles, or multi-tenancy.

nodes.label: One Process and One SQLite File
nodes.rationale: The simplest deploy and backup model is one Node process with a persistent SQLite file.
nodes.norm_label: one process and one sqlite file

nodes.label: Compute Derived Fields on Write
nodes.rationale: Persisting expiry and USD results makes dashboards fast, indexable, and consistent instead of duplicating calculations across reads.

nodes.label: Payment-Time FX Snapshot
nodes.rationale: Recorded payments are historical facts, so rate_used, rate_as_of, source, and USD minor amount must remain stable after market rates change.

nodes.label: Exchange-Rate Anti-Corruption Layer
nodes.rationale: Only one service knows the provider shape, owns cache writes, and supplies last-good fallback.

nodes.label: Currency-Aware Integer Minor Units
nodes.rationale: Integer amounts plus ISO 4217 exponents prevent float drift and preserve zero- and three-decimal currencies.

nodes.label: Calendar-Aware Expiry Arithmetic
nodes.rationale: Subscription dates need month-end and leap-day clamping and local calendar-date comparison, never thirty-day or millisecond arithmetic.

nodes.label: Stable Channel FK and Soft Delete
nodes.rationale: Channel IDs preserve attribution across renames while soft delete prevents orphaned historical spaces.
nodes.norm_label: stable channel fk and soft delete

nodes.label: Transactional Space Account Cascade
nodes.rationale: Deleting a space must remove its mother and child accounts atomically so counts and joins cannot retain orphans.

nodes.label: Renewal Risk Plus Total USD Core Value
nodes.norm_label: renewal risk plus total usd core value

nodes.label: Last-Good FX Cache and Manual Fallback
nodes.rationale: FX outages must not erase rates, write zero values, or break the dashboard.
nodes.norm_label: last-good fx cache and manual fallback

nodes.label: Email and IM Notifications Deferred
nodes.rationale: Notification scheduling and deliverability were initially treated as v2 complexity compared with dashboard-only renewal signals.

nodes.label: No Child Account Credentials
nodes.rationale: The tool stores login identifiers only and deliberately excludes passwords and secrets.

nodes.label: RSC Reads and Server Actions Mutate
nodes.rationale: Server Components query SQLite directly for read-heavy views while Server Actions keep CRUD in the same application.
nodes.norm_label: rsc reads and server actions mutate

nodes.label: Space Spend and Rented Child Revenue Separation
nodes.norm_label: space spend and rented child revenue separation

nodes.label: Separate Space Child and Mother Aggregate Queries
nodes.norm_label: separate space child and mother aggregate queries

nodes.label: Country Currency Channel and Space Distributions
nodes.norm_label: country currency channel and space distributions

nodes.label: USD Rate Pinned to Decimal String One
nodes.norm_label: usd rate pinned to decimal string one

nodes.label: SQLite WAL and Foreign Key Connection Policy
nodes.norm_label: sqlite wal and foreign key connection policy

nodes.label: Programmatic Drizzle Migration Runner
nodes.rationale: Creates the database directory, enables foreign keys, applies only unapplied committed migrations, and closes the SQLite handle.

nodes.label: Reminder Delivery Unique Indexes
nodes.rationale: Child delivery is unique by child, due date, and recipient; space delivery is unique by space, expiry date, and threshold.

nodes.label: Space and Account Cascade Relations
nodes.rationale: Mother, child, and reminder records are removed through foreign-key cascades when their owning entity is deleted.

nodes.label: Thirty Frankfurter-Supported Currency Records
nodes.rationale: The catalog centralizes code, name, symbol, country mapping, and minor-unit exponent for every supported currency.

nodes.label: Explicit Database Handle Query Pattern
nodes.rationale: Query helpers accept a database argument so production and migration-backed in-memory tests exercise the same builders.

nodes.label: Configurable Space and Child Status Thresholds
nodes.norm_label: configurable space and child status thresholds

nodes.label: Legacy Child Reminder Template Upgrade
nodes.rationale: Known legacy HTML bodies are replaced with the current default template when settings are read.

nodes.label: Key-Value Settings Upsert Repository
nodes.rationale: All settings persist as independent app_setting keys through conflict-update writes.

nodes.label: Exact Threshold-Day Space Reminder Policy
nodes.rationale: Candidates cover non-expired dates inside the window, but outbound due reminders fire only at exactly the configured threshold and only once.

nodes.label: Exact-Name Cascade Delete Confirmation
nodes.rationale: Deletion returns not_found or name_mismatch without touching data and removes the space only after exact-name confirmation.

nodes.label: HMAC-SHA256 Session Signature
nodes.rationale: Versioned session payloads are signed with APP_AUTH_SECRET and compared without an early-exit string comparison.

nodes.label: In-Memory Login Rate Limiter
nodes.rationale: Each identifier receives a five-failure rolling window and fifteen-minute lock, cleared after successful login.

nodes.label: countryOptionsFromCurrencies
nodes.rationale: Selectable country options come from DB-backed currency metadata, are deduplicated by code, and are sorted for Chinese display.

nodes.label: Calendar Month-End and Leap-Day Clamping
nodes.rationale: date-fns calendar adds and payment-day clamping preserve billing dates across short months and leap years.

nodes.label: BigInt Round-Half-Up Conversion
nodes.rationale: Both FX directions parse positive decimal strings into integer ratios and perform exponent scaling and half-up rounding without floating-point arithmetic.

nodes.label: Space Expiry Reminder Email Test Suite
nodes.norm_label: space expiry reminder email test suite

nodes.label: Any Supported Base Normalizes to X-to-USD
nodes.norm_label: any supported base normalizes to x-to-usd

nodes.label: Failure Leaves Cache Untouched and Falls Back
nodes.norm_label: failure leaves cache untouched and falls back
